stem: A climate monitoring study measures daily temperature readings for 25 samples to determine if the process variability is within ±0.5°C of the calibration standard. The sample standard deviation is 2.05 °C (sample variance = 4.22). Estimate the true population standard deviation of daily temperature readings and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Sample SD = 2.05 °C. 95% CI for σ (chi-squared): [1.6, 2.86]. Point estimate: 2.05 °C.

common_failure_modes: Using z-based CI for the mean instead of χ²-based CI for the variance
Reporting the sample SD (2.05) as the population SD without uncertainty
Not recognizing that confidence intervals for variance are asymmetric
